ai._description: Anthropic API settings used by all data refresh scripts.

_meta.description: NZF Dashboard Business Rules — single source of truth for all dashboard logic. Update this file to change how any metric is defined. Python scripts import this directly. Upload this file to Claude project context so Claude always has NZF business definitions when building new dashboards.

zoho_modules._description: Mapping of NZF terms to Zoho CRM API module names. Zoho uses generic names; NZF has renamed them.

case_notes_joined._description: Pre-built Analytics query table — Cases + Distributions + Notes already joined
case_notes_joined.view_name: Cases x Distribution x Notes - All

client_definitions._description: How clients are classified for reporting purposes

new_client.definition: A client whose case in the reporting window is their first ever case across all time — no prior cases of any status or outcome.
new_client.logic: Compare case Created Time against the earliest case date for that client across the full Cases table. If this case IS the earliest, the client is New.
new_client.month_attribution: The month of the case Created Time field is used to attribute the client to a reporting month.
notes: A client rejected on their first application is still New — case outcome does not affect classification.
The full Cases table (not just the reporting window) must be used to determine first-ever case.

returning_client.definition: A client who has at least one prior case and has submitted a genuine new application — not a continuation of the same instance of assistance.
returning_client.logic: Client has earlier cases on record AND the new case passes the same-instance exclusion checks.
returning_client.month_attribution: The month of the new case Created Time field is used.
notes: Prior case outcome is irrelevant — a client who was previously not funded and reapplies is still Returning.
Same-instance cases (ILA payments, re-opened cases, follow-up instalments) are excluded from both New and Returning counts.

same_instance_exclusions._description: Cases that are continuations of a previous assistance instance, not genuine new applications. These are excluded from both New and Returning counts.
same_instance_exclusions.definition: A case is same-instance if it represents ongoing, linked, or instalment-based assistance from a prior approval — not a new request for help.
notes: Keywords are checked against the case Description field (case-insensitive).
Add new keywords here as new ILA or continuation patterns are identified.
If a caseworker identifies a pattern not covered, add the keyword here — no code change needed.

distributions._description: Rules for how distributions (payments to clients) are classified

paid_date_logic.definition: The effective payment date for a distribution.
rules: If Status = 'Paid'      → use Paid Date field
If Status = 'Extracted' → use Extracted Date field
If the specific date field is blank → fall back to Created Time

distributions._amount_note: Grand Total field — contains AU$ prefix that must be stripped before parsing as float

return_gap_bands.definition: How time-since-last-assistance is grouped for reporting

case_performance._description: Rules for the Cases Performance dashboard — response time, closure time, SLA targets, automated note exclusions.

sla_response._description: Response SLA — time from case creation to first genuine caseworker note.

sla_resolution._description: Resolution SLA — time from case creation to case closure or first paid distribution.

working_hours._description: Used for P1 response SLA only. Clock pauses outside 9AM–5PM Mon–Fri AEST.

automated_note_titles._description: Note titles that represent system-generated or intake admin notes — NOT genuine caseworker actions. These are excluded from first-response time calculations. Add new patterns here as they are identified.

case_performance._trend_note: Number of months used for trend comparison (last N vs prior N months)

case_priorities._description: Normalisation rules for the Case Urgency field. Raw values in Zoho CRM are inconsistent — these prefix matches normalise them to clean labels for reporting.

classification_guide._description: Used by AI to assess whether priority assignments match case descriptions.
classification_guide.P1: 24-hour turnaround. Critical Risk — Life-Threatening Needs: homelessness or immediate risk of rough sleeping, urgent food assistance, emergency medication needs, funeral/burial costs, other urgent emergencies impacting survival or safety. Domestic violence is P1 ONLY when the client is currently experiencing active abuse, is in immediate danger, or faces imminent homelessness as a direct result — historical DV without current danger should follow normal priority rules.
classification_guide.P2: 2-7 day turnaround. High Risk of Harm Within Days: imminent eviction (within days), emergency or essential utility disconnection (gas, electricity), basic necessities at crisis level (baby formula, heating, hygiene), single parent with children in acute financial crisis, rent arrears with landlord threatening immediate action.
classification_guide.P3: 2-week turnaround. Moderate Risk — Stable to Short Term: no immediate impact in next 14 days, financial support or bills that are manageable temporarily, general financial hardship, food insecurity not immediately acute, employment loss, general rental stress.
classification_guide.P4: 1-month turnaround. Low Risk — Long-Term/Deferred Needs: long-term debt repayment, non-essential bills or expenses not affecting client in the next month, general assistance planning, documentation support, non-urgent enquiries.
classification_guide.No Priority: Priority not yet assigned — should be assigned within 24 hours of application.

case_stages._description: Zoho CRM case stage definitions and groupings for reporting

stage_groups._description: Logical groupings of stages for dashboard summaries
intake: Ready for Allocation: Potential Fast Track

reporting_periods._description: Standard time windows used across all reports

zakat_categories._description: The eight Quranic categories of Zakat eligibility used by NZF
categories: In the Way of Allah (Fi Sabilillah)

dashboard_notes._description: Context for Claude when building or modifying dashboards
dashboard_notes.brand_guide: See NZF_WebsiteFigma_Brand.png in project files for colours, typography and component styles.
dashboard_notes.audience: NZF internal caseworkers, team leads and management — not public-facing
dashboard_notes.data_sensitivity: All case data is sensitive. No client names or personal details should appear in aggregated reports.
dashboard_notes.auth_status: Entra ID SSO hooks are built in but currently disabled. See assets/js/auth.js AUTH_ENABLED flag.
